title: Boat Rental in Nice: Exploring the Côte d'Azur in 2026
html:
For those seeking to explore the Côte d'Azur by water, renting a boat in Nice offers unmatched access to secluded coves and glittering bays. Departing from the historic Port de Nice, you can charter anything from a small motorboat for around €300 per half-day to a crewed yacht upwards of €2,000. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators.

Where to set off in Nice

Nice offers several departure points for your boating adventure. The main hub is Port de Nice, a large marina with ample berthing for charter boats. Nearby, Port Lympia (the historic port) provides a picturesque start with its pastel buildings. For a more exclusive feel, Beaulieu-sur-Mer and Villefranche-sur-Mer are just a short sail east, offering calmer waters. The Baie des Anges is the iconic bay stretching from Nice to Cap Ferrat, perfect for a leisurely cruise. Westward, Antibes and the Îles de Lérins (off Cannes) are popular day-trip destinations, approximately 12 nautical miles from Nice.

Which boat to rent in Nice

Choosing the right boat depends on your plans and group size. Here is a breakdown of popular options available on BoatHire24. In the motorboat category, you can compare the Azimut 58 for a luxury day cruise with up to 12 guests.

  1. Motorboat (6-8 pax) Best for short coastal hops, from €350 half-day.
  2. Sailing yacht (8-10 pax) Ideal for a relaxed day sail with wind in the sails, from €500 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (10-12 pax) Perfect for groups seeking stability and space, from €800 full-day.
  4. Crewed yacht (max 12 pax) Full service with skipper and crew, from €1,500 full-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Nice?

Prices vary by vessel size, season, and duration, but expect from €300 to over €2,000 for a full day.

  • Shared group tours: from €80 per person for a 3-hour sightseeing trip.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 guests): around €350.
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 12 guests): approximately €900.
  • Crewed yacht (full-day, up to 12 guests): from €1,500, often including fuel and crew.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Nice?

French law requires a valid boating licence for vessels over 6 metres or with engines above a certain power; the operator will confirm exact requirements. Many charter companies in Nice offer skippered rentals, so no licence is needed if you hire a skipper. BoatHire24 provides clarity on each listing, and you can browse boats for your dates with confidence. For a genuine local tip, consider this: in high season (July and August), the prevailing Mistral wind can create choppy conditions in the Baie des Anges, so early morning departures are advisable for calmer seas.

In July and August, book your boat well in advance as the Mistral wind can disrupt afternoon outings; always check the local forecast before heading out.

At a glance

  • Price band: €300, €2,000 per day
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Trip length: Half-day (4h) to full-day (8h)
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 passengers
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Nice

Can I rent a boat in Nice without a licence?

Yes, by choosing a skippered charter, the captain handles all navigation, so no licence is required from you.

What is the best time of year for boating in Nice?

The best conditions are from May to September, with July and August offering the warmest weather and water temperatures above 22°C.

Are there any hidden costs on a boat hire in Nice?

Fuel and dock fees are often extra; always confirm what is included in the rental price before booking.

Can I overnight on a rented boat in Nice?

Overnight stays are possible on larger crewed yachts but usually require specific arrangements; check with the operator in advance.

To understand the booking process and what to expect, read how boat rental works on the platform. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Nice or list your own. Whether you own a vessel or are planning a trip for 2026, check availability on the website. If you have a boat to share, consider becoming a host to earn income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Nice and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
